verify.value: All required fields are present.
verify.rule: All specified fields must be included in the blueprint.
The mass estimate must be a numeric value and within the expected range.

verify.value: All P0 internal_feature_targets are mapped to geometry_ops.
verify.rule: Each internal_feature_target must have corresponding geometry_ops.

verify.value: All interfaces have matching required_features in neighboring subsystems.
verify.rule: Each interface must have a corresponding feature in the neighboring subsystem.

verify.value: Visible insert/washer/locking/preload evidence is present for critical joints.
verify.rule: Critical joints must have visible locking or preload evidence.
Boundary conditions must be defined for downstream CFD/FEA.

risk.desc: Potential misalignment during assembly due to lack of datum reference.
risk.mit: Ensure a primary datum feature is explicitly named and placed.

risk.desc: Risk of strain relief failure if cable exits are not properly supported.
risk.mit: Include strain relief geometry (clamp, grommet, tie point) for every cable exit.

risk.desc: Risk of incorrect mating if connectors are not keyed or labeled.
risk.mit: Ensure connectors have keying or labeling to prevent mis-mating.

risk.desc: Risk of assembly errors if the assembly sequence is not clearly defined.
risk.mit: Include an ordered assembly_sequence with tool, torque, and datum per step.

risk.desc: Risk of overclaiming if verification focuses on real launch readiness.
risk.mit: Ensure verification focuses on mockup structure, inspection, and interfaces.

physics_paths.description: A visual path through the cutaway window to view the combustion chamber dummy and turbopump assembly dummy.
A thermal path from the combustion chamber dummy to the bell nozzle shell for educational display.
A path for human access to install and remove the display dummy via the slide rail and bolt circle dummy.

risk.desc: Potential misalignment during assembly due to loose fit tolerances
risk.mit: Specify tighter fit classes and conduct pre-assembly fit checks

risk.desc: Risk of accidental mis-mating of connectors due to lack of keying
risk.mit: Ensure all connectors are keyed or labeled to prevent mis-mating

risk.desc: Thermal management could be compromised if heat sink fins are not properly aligned
risk.mit: Include alignment features for heat sink fins and conduct thermal testing

risk.desc: Risk of structural failure if bolt preload is not properly applied
risk.mit: Specify torque values and conduct preload verification during assembly

risk.desc: Potential for cable strain if bend radius is not respected
risk.mit: Ensure all cable exits have strain relief features and routing respects minimum bend radius

requirements: Educational tray with board_standoff, harness_passthrough, connector labels, EMI shield mock
No ordnance or flight computer authority
Avionics ring/tray with board mounts and harness pass-throughs
Avionics tray must be removable without full teardown
Harnesses, tubes, or service channels are routed through visible raceways or protected channels
Connectors are reachable without disassembling structure
Every cable exit shows strain relief geometry
Connectors have finger/tool clearance and mention keying or labeling to prevent mis-mating
P0 internal_feature_targets appear as part_tree children and geometry_ops ids or targets
Most geometry_ops include args.at coordinates distributed across the local subsystem volume
Uses drill, pocket, boss, channel, pattern_polar, fillet, chamfer
Every part_tree child has 4+ dedicated geometry_ops
A primary datum feature (flat, bore, or pin) is explicitly named and placed
Adjacent interfaces include fit_class (H7/h6, LC2) and stack_up_mm
Ordered assembly_sequence with tool, torque, datum per step
Uses seed-characteristic ops: chamfer, subtract, pocket, drill
Dimensions match small_launch_vehicle scale: 100-500mm dia, 500-2000mm length
Wall thickness, hole diameters, fillet radii are physically plausible
